title: Fix hang in apiv2 test_connect
body:
When executing on F36, starting the podman service in debug-mode causes aardvark to run in debug mode.  This does unexpected things with file-descriptors leading to a test-hang.  Thanks to @Luap99 for the fix.  See also https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/128 and https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13934

Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13932

comments.body: This should be good to go.
Force-pushed: Fixed commits and removed timeouts for @TomSweeneyRedHat per https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376#pullrequestreview-949312922

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
CI seems to be examining its navel fuzz.  I've just approved the PR  in hopes it will get out of its revelry.

LGTM
and happy green test buttons!
